breach.summary: Major credit reporting agency experienced large-scale breach exposing Social Security numbers, dates of birth, and credit card information.
Hotel chain breach exposed guest payment cards and personal information across multiple properties via compromised reservation system.
Financial services company experienced breach affecting customers and credit card applicants due to misconfigured firewall.
Major retailer's point-of-sale systems compromised during holiday shopping season, exposing millions of customer payment cards.
Title insurance company exposed sensitive financial documents including bank account numbers and social security numbers.
Health insurance provider experienced breach affecting members nationwide, exposing personal and medical information.
Email platform breach compromised nearly 3 billion user accounts, one of the largest data breaches in history.
Retailer's point-of-sale systems exposed customer payment card information through malware installed at checkout terminals.
Insurance company experienced breach exposing employee and customer personal information through email compromise.
Social media platform exposed user phone numbers and personal data through misconfigured API endpoints.
Healthcare provider left unencrypted patient records in publicly accessible S3 bucket for 3 weeks, exposing names, SSNs, and medical histories.
Threat actor gained access to Okta's support system, potentially exposing customer metadata and session tokens used for multifactor authentication.
Remote Code Execution vulnerability in Exchange Server allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code and access mailboxes.
Exposed driver personal information including names, phone numbers, email addresses, and vehicle details on misconfigured database.
Tax information and payment details for Twitch streamers leaked through compromised database, affecting creators worldwide.
Threat actors used stolen API keys to access and retrieve signed documents containing personal and financial information.
Misconfigured sharing settings exposed internal company wikis, roadmaps, and credentials in multiple Notion workspaces.
Multiple e-commerce stores on Shopify compromised via weak admin passwords, leading to customer payment card theft.
Developers left MongoDB instances publicly accessible without authentication, exposing customer databases with PII.
Legacy Slack bot tokens exposed in GitHub repositories, allowing attackers to access private channels and user data.

The D1R cybercrime group claimed to have stolen valuable data from Synopsys and Bosch, threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. The post Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims

breach.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps...
breach.org: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak

breach.summary: Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to pay $18 million to settle claims from a coalition of 43 attorneys general that it failed to protect customers' genetic data. [...]
breach.org: 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement

breach.summary: A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages. This on-demand webinar reveals how this Approval Gap forms, and gives your team the blueprint to close it before an auditor, regulator, or...
breach.org: New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech

breach.summary: Ernst & Young is notifying customers of a data breach caused by the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT personnel. [...]
breach.org: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack

cve.summary: Remote code execution vulnerability in Cisco IOS XE allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain EXEC access.
Authentication bypass in Confluence allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.
SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer affecting millions of users worldwide.
Heap-based buffer overflow in FortiOS allowing remote code execution on firewalls.
Authentication bypass in SolarWinds Orion Platform allowing unauthorized access to monitoring systems.
Remote code execution in vCenter Server affecting virtualized infrastructure globally.
Command injection in PAN-OS allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands on firewalls.
Remote code execution in Microsoft Exchange Server allowing unauthenticated attackers to compromise email systems.
OGNL injection vulnerability in Apache Struts allowing remote code execution in web applications.
Privilege escalation vulnerability in IBM Db2 allowing local users to gain administrative access.
Remote Code Execution in Log4j 2.x via JNDI injection in logging messages. CVSS 10.0.
Use-after-free vulnerability in V8 JavaScript engine allowing arbitrary code execution. Exploit available.
Privilege escalation in Windows Kernel allowing local users to gain SYSTEM access. Active exploitation detected.
Buffer overflow in OpenSSL certificate validation allowing DoS and potential RCE. Affects versions 1.1.1 and 3.0.x.
Type confusion vulnerability in PHP's filter functions allowing information disclosure and RCE.
Prototype pollution vulnerability in jQuery allowing XSS attacks in web applications.
Race condition in Linux kernel memory management allowing privilege escalation on multi-core systems.
HTTP request smuggling vulnerability in nginx HTTP/2 module allowing request injection attacks.
New CPU microarchitecture side-channel attack (Spectre variant) affecting Intel processors from 2010-2026.
Information disclosure in AWS Lambda environment variables leaking database credentials and API keys.
CISA has ordered federal agencies to secure their systems by Saturday against ongoing attacks exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Oracle E-Business Suite financial application. [...]
cve.cve_id: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Oracle flaw by

cve.summary: A Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" used Google's open-source Gemini CLI AI tool as a hacking agent and to operate a small-scale botnet. [...]
cve.cve_id: Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet

cve.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]
cve.cve_id: CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint fla

cve.summary: Pull the certificate off the flash of a Shark RV2320EDUS robot vacuum, and you can run root commands on other people's Shark vacuums across the same AWS region: watch the camera, drive the robot, read the map of the house, and take the Wi-Fi password in plaintext. A researcher publishing under the handle tokay0 put...
cve.cve_id: Unpatched Shark Vacuum Flaw Could Let Attackers Control Othe

cve.summary: OpenAI has disclosed details of GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model that scales prompt injection vulnerability discovery with an aim to fix issues before the tools are deployed widely. "GPT‑Red is a strong red-teamer, and our previous models are highly vulnerable to its prompt injection attacks," the...
cve.cve_id: OpenAI’s GPT-Red Automates Prompt Injection Testing to Harde

cve.summary: Open a repository in Cursor on Windows and, if a file named git.exe is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. No click, no approval dialog, no warning that anything in the folder is about to execute. Whatever that binary does, it does as you, with your source, your SSH keys and your cloud tokens. Cursor keeps...
cve.cve_id: Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windo

cve.summary: The flaws could allow attackers to access credentials and data, take over accounts, and escalate their privileges. The post Splunk, Zoom Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

The researcher stripped the proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit to prevent immediate exploitation of the vulnerability. The post Nightmare Eclipse Drops ‘LegacyHive’ Windows Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

cve.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup

The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS score: 10.0) - A Server-side request forgery...

cve.summary: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a previously unknown WordPress plugin zero-day, with additional discoveries already under responsible...
cve.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out

cve.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by July 19, 2026. The...
cve.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV

cve.title: Fresh SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited Soon After Disclosure

cve.summary: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]
cve.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges

The activity allows users to enumerate user accounts and validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments, without ever generating a...

"LabubaRAT creates a reusable foothold for hands-on activity," Blackpoint Cyber researchers Sam Decker and Nevan Beal said...
threat.actor: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts

threat.actor: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools

threat.actor: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet

The packages did not go after the developers who might install them. The operators used the registry as free...
threat.actor: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet

"The malware is full-featured, lightweight, and modular," Elastic Security Labs researcher Cyril François said in a technical report. "While...
threat.actor: New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands

threat.summary: ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for the password behind a fake system dialog, and when the victim cancels, installs two LaunchAgents and quietly exits....
threat.actor: New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password

threat.summary: Five malicious versions of AsyncAPI packages were published to the Node Package Manager (npm) in a supply-chain attack that delivered a remote access trojan with info-stealing capabilities. [...]
threat.actor: AsyncAPI npm packages infected with credential-stealing malware

threat.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance from a large language model (LLM), albeit with not so successful results. "While the AI complied with their request to...
threat.actor: TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development

threat.summary: A malware framework called OkoBot has been running on Windows machines since April 2025, and one of its modules is built to con hardware wallet owners out of their recovery phrase. On an infected PC, the request comes from inside the wallet's own desktop software. Sometimes it waits until you plug the device in...
threat.actor: OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps

threat.summary: A modular implant borrows from various malware families to combine both backdoor and wiper activities to maximize impact and minimize operational output.
threat.actor: GigaWiper Lets Threat Actors Choose Their Own Destructive Attack

threat.summary: An advanced malware previously attributed to a China-linked threat actor has resurfaced after more than four years within a Taiwan manufacturing firm, along with a previously unreported backdoor dubbed Stupig. Daxin ("srt64.sys"), as the kernel-mode rootkit is referred to, was first documented by Broadcom-owned...
threat.actor: Daxin Resurfaces in Taiwan Alongside Stupig Pre-Login SYSTEM Backdoor

threat.actor: ‘ClickLock Stealer’ Bypasses macOS Security With Social Engineering, Process Killing

This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to...

The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about...

threat.summary: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced OneDrive and SharePoint folders. It gets in because someone pasted a command into a Run box and pressed Enter....

Russian cybersecurity company Kaspersky, which uncovered the...

threat.summary: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with ComfyUI, Ollama, n8n, Open WebUI, Langflow, and Gradio: the image generators, local model runners, and workflow builders...
threat.actor: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens

threat.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event, described the threat actor as a sub-group of GoldenEyeDog (aka APT-Q-27, Dragon Breath, and Miuuti Group), a Chinese...
threat.actor: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft

threat.summary: North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed employing steganography in SVG image files to conceal malicious payloads as part of a campaign using fake job postings and coding challenges. "Any user who ran the project ended up with a four-stage payload aligned with...
threat.actor: Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images

news.title: Biden Signs Executive Order on Cybersecurity
news.summary: President Biden signed comprehensive cybersecurity executive order strengthening critical infrastructure protection.

news.title: NIST Publishes Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
news.summary: National Institute of Standards and Technology released updated cybersecurity framework with new controls.

news.title: Cybersecurity Regulation Tightens Globally
news.summary: European Union and other nations implement stricter cybersecurity regulations for critical infrastructure operators.

news.title: Supply Chain Attacks Double in 2025
news.summary: Report shows supply chain attacks increased 200% in 2025, with SolarWinds-style incidents becoming more common.

news.title: Cloud Security Spending Surges
news.summary: Cloud security market experiencing 30% annual growth as enterprises invest heavily in protection for cloud environments.

news.title: Incident Response as a Service Grows
news.summary: Managed incident response services seeing increased adoption as organizations struggle with cyber incident response.

news.title: Ransomware Demands Hit $30M Average
news.summary: Report indicates average ransomware ransom demand increased to $30 million, targeting only highest-value targets.

news.title: MFA Fatigue Attacks on Rise
news.summary: Attackers increasingly exploiting user fatigue with multi-factor authentication prompts to gain account access.

news.title: Identity and Access Management Critical
news.summary: Security experts emphasize IAM as critical control point for defending against modern cyber threats.

news.title: Zero Trust Adoption Accelerates
news.summary: More organizations moving to zero-trust security models after high-profile breaches demonstrate perimeter defense failures.

news.title: CISA Releases New Security Guidance
news.summary: CISA published updated secure software development framework addressing supply chain security and zero-trust architecture.

news.title: EU Digital Services Act Enforcement Begins
news.summary: European Union begins enforcement of Digital Services Act with first round of compliance checks on major tech platforms.

news.title: Quantum Computing Threatens Encryption
news.summary: New quantum computer achieves cryptographically relevant breakthrough, prompting urgent migration to post-quantum cryptography.

news.title: AI-Powered Phishing Attacks Surge
news.summary: Cybersecurity firms report 400% increase in sophisticated phishing emails generated by large language models.

news.title: Zero-Trust Architecture Adoption Growing
news.summary: Gartner reports 60% of enterprises now implementing zero-trust network architecture as default security model.

news.title: Ransomware Payments Hit Record High
news.summary: Chainalysis reports ransomware payments exceeded $2.5 billion in 2025, with healthcare sector accounting for 32% of payments.

news.title: Password-Less Authentication Adoption
news.summary: Microsoft reports 50% of new Azure AD logins now use passwordless authentication methods like Windows Hello.

news.title: IoT Botnet Reaches 15M Devices
news.summary: New Mirai variant infecting 15 million IoT devices worldwide, used for DDoS and cryptocurrency mining operations.

news.title: Cybersecurity Skills Shortage Worsens
news.summary: Industry report shows 3.5M cybersecurity job openings unfilled globally, leading to burnout among existing security professionals.

news.title: Bug Bounty Programs Break Records
news.summary: HackerOne and Bugcrowd combined disbursed over $500M in bug bounties, with white-hat hackers finding critical flaws at scale.

kev.action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

kev.summary: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 contains an overly restrictive account lockout mechanism vulnerability that could allow an attacker to purge all devices without additional security options enabled and set a BCU key to lock the device.
